recommendation_summary: Northgate may proceed toward a locally-trained ESM v2 deployment only through a silent-shadow then single-unit supervised pilot, gated on local validation, leakage audit, equity stratification, and a monitoring/decommissioning plan; house-wide live alerting as posed is not approved.
rationale: The session advanced to deploy_with_conditions because the affirmative case is genuinely real (E2's high base-rate preventable sepsis mortality makes 'do nothing' unsafe, and E3 shows the vendor redesigned the model to address the documented failure mechanisms) yet every favorable performance number is unusable as-is: E1's strong figures are single-center, before/after, and version-unspecified; E4 and E5 are damning but pertain to v1; and E7 establishes there is no v2 deployed-performance evidence at all. Chose conditioned-staged deployment over outright do_not_deploy because the evidence against deployment is overwhelmingly against v1, not v2, and rejecting on v1 evidence would forfeit E2's base-rate benefit on a version mismatch. Chose conditioned-staged over deploy because no artifact yet shows the locally trained v2 works, is additive over clinician recognition (E6 leakage), or is equitable (E5), so live alerting before a local validation gate would be unsupported by evidence. The core tradeoff: accept a bounded delay (gates G1-G4) over both an evidence-free live deployment and a version-confused rejection.
proposal_defended: ESM v2 trained on Northgate's own historical data (per E3); staged rollout = silent/shadow scoring (no alerts) -> local validation study -> single supervised pilot unit with alerts as decision support (not autonomous) -> house-wide only after gates lift; alert threshold to be calibrated to local prevalence rather than the vendor default 6 that failed in E4; safeguards = lead-time/leakage audit, equity stratification, monitoring dashboard, recalibration cadence, and pre-defined decommissioning criteria.
conditions: G1 Local validation gate: no clinician-facing alert fires until a Northgate-data v2 study reports discrimination, calibration, and threshold-specific operating characteristics on a held-out local cohort against a pre-registered performance floor.
G2 Leakage/lead-time audit: shadow-phase evidence that v2 adds warning ahead of the antibiotic order rather than echoing it.
G3 Equity stratification: subgroup performance (race, ethnicity, language, payer) reported and acted on before pilot scale-up.
G4 Ops package: monitoring dashboard, recalibration cadence, alert-fatigue governance, and a decommissioning/kill-switch threshold live and owned before any pilot alert.
Model is strictly additive: a 'no alert' never justifies withholding clinician escalation; existing sepsis workflow preserved unchanged throughout.
Gating timeline is bounded so conditioning does not itself become a cause of treatment delay (E2).

assumptions.text: Northgate has the data infrastructure and vendor support to train and locally validate ESM v2 on its own historical encounters, as E3 says Epic recommends.
Northgate currently runs sepsis care via clinician recognition and a sepsis program; the model would augment, not replace, that workflow.
A silent/shadow deployment phase that scores patients without firing clinician alerts is operationally feasible and carries no direct patient risk.

claims.text: Deploy ESM v2 trained on Northgate's own data, not the v1 configuration that failed external validation.
The redesign targeted the exact mechanisms blamed for v1's failures (sepsis-onset definition, reduced antibiotic-order reliance), so v2 is the right candidate.

A single-center before/after deployment was associated with strong operating characteristics (AUC 0.834, sens 86%) and a 44% reduction in odds of sepsis mortality, showing the deployment archetype can help.
Local training plus a locally calibrated threshold addresses the population-mismatch and default-threshold problems seen in the external validations.
Deployed as additive decision support in a supervised pilot, the model can add early warning without replacing clinician judgment.
A shadow phase plus monitoring lets Northgate verify performance before any live alert and catch drift after.
Given the base-rate burden of sepsis, having an early-warning capability is worth pursuing rather than abandoning.

objections.failure_mode: No independent or vendor performance evidence exists for v2; the case rests entirely on design intent (E3) while the only hard external numbers (E4, E5) are v1 and are poor.
objections.consequence: Deploying live alerting on an unvalidated model could fire clinically misleading alerts at unknown sensitivity/PPV.
objections.verification: Local validation study of locally-trained v2 on a held-out Northgate cohort reporting discrimination, calibration, and threshold-specific operating characteristics (G1).

objections.failure_mode: E1's favorable AUC/sensitivity come from a single-center, before/after design with model version unspecified and authors calling it 'hypothesis generating'; it cannot be attributed to v2 or transferred to Northgate.
objections.consequence: Overstating expected benefit and importing a number that may belong to a different model/population.
objections.verification: Version confirmation of the E1 study and a local before/after or prospective metric from Northgate's own pilot.
objections.resolution_text: Advocate conceded E1 cannot justify live deployment and demoted it to supporting plausibility only; the live-go decision now rests on local validation (G1), not E1.

objections.failure_mode: v1 used clinician antibiotic-order timing as a predictor (target leakage), producing late/circular alerts; E3 says v2 only 'reduces' reliance, not eliminates it, and the v2 feature set at Northgate is undocumented.
objections.consequence: Alerts may fire only after clinicians have acted, yielding near-zero additive lead time as in E4's 7% figure.
objections.verification: Shadow-phase lead-time/leakage audit and documentation of the v2 feature inputs used locally (G2).

objections.failure_mode: E1 does not specify model version, an unclosable provenance gap on that data point.
objections.consequence: Cannot rely on E1 as evidence for any specific version's deployed performance.
objections.verification: Author/source confirmation of the version used in the Prisma study.
objections.resolution_text: Advocate accepted the lineage discipline and withdrew E1 as version-specific evidence, logging it as version-unspecified; the v2 case rests on E3 plus the local-validation gate, not on E1.

objections.failure_mode: v1 performed worst (sensitivity 14.7%) in a predominantly Hispanic/Black, lower-SES population (E5); local v2 performance across Northgate's subpopulations is unmeasured.
objections.consequence: Inequitable miss rates could systematically under-protect specific patient groups.
objections.verification: Equity-stratified performance report from the local validation cohort (G3).

objections.failure_mode: With v1 missing 67% of sepsis (E4), a 'no alert' could be misread as reassurance and delay escalation.
objections.consequence: False reassurance harm to patients the model misses.
objections.verification: Pilot protocol mandating additive-only use and tracking of any escalation deferred on a negative score.
objections.resolution_text: Advocate accepted as a binding amendment that the model is decision support only and a negative score never justifies withholding clinician escalation; existing workflow preserved. This removes the false-reassurance mechanism by design rather than leaving it to clinician discretion.

objections.failure_mode: E4 shows an 18% alert rate at 12% PPV — roughly 7 of 8 alerts non-sepsis — a classic alert-fatigue load.
objections.consequence: Clinicians ignore/dismiss alerts, eroding any real benefit and adding workflow burden.
objections.verification: Alert-fatigue governance: locally calibrated threshold, override/dismissal tracking, and a single-unit pilot to measure fatigue before scaling.
objections.resolution_text: Advocate accepted threshold calibration to local prevalence (rejecting the vendor default 6 that produced E4's numbers) plus mandatory override/dismissal tracking in the single-unit pilot, converting an open fatigue risk into a measured, governed pilot metric.

objections.failure_mode: No monitoring, recalibration cadence, or decommissioning criteria exist yet; E7 confirms the evidence to operate v2 safely must be generated, and a model can drift silently after go-live.
objections.consequence: Performance decay or miscalibration goes undetected; no defined off-ramp.
objections.verification: Ops package: live monitoring dashboard, recalibration cadence, decommissioning threshold, named owner (G4).

objections.failure_mode: The forming consensus (gate-then-pilot) risks being a permanent stall dressed as caution; institutions rarely complete local validation, so 'deploy_with_conditions' can become de-facto do_not_deploy.
objections.consequence: The base-rate sepsis mortality benefit (E2) is never realized because the gates never close.
objections.verification: Bounded gating timeline with a named owner and slip signal.
objections.resolution_text: Defended by bounding the gate timeline, naming owners, and preserving the existing clinician sepsis workflow throughout so no patient is worse off during gating than under the status quo; the conditions add a capability without removing the current one.

objections.failure_mode: Arguing the cost of NON-deployment: delay and excess caution themselves cost lives given E2's burden, and an imperfect-but-additive tool withheld is a harm.
objections.consequence: Patients who could have benefited from any early warning go without while the institution validates.
objections.verification: Demonstration that the shadow phase delivers no patient-facing change vs. status quo and that the gate timeline is short.

minority_reports.text: Devil's-advocate minority position (survives as a tension, not a veto): the evidence the conditioned recommendation leans on to justify proceeding at all (E1, E2, E3) is weaker than the evidence it leans on to refuse (E4, E5, E6, E7). E1 is single-center/before-after/version-unspecified; E3 is design intent with explicitly zero performance figures; E2 establishes that sepsis is deadly but the packet itself warns E2 gives no dose-response tie between time-to-treatment and survival, so it cannot quantify the benefit of THIS tool. Meanwhile two independent external validations (E4, E5) show the named product failing, including worst in a minority/low-income population, and E7 confirms the proposed v2 has no validation at all. A defensible reading is do_not_deploy until v2 validation exists somewhere independent — i.e., do not let Northgate be the first published validation of a live patient-facing tool. The conditioned recommendation answers this by making Northgate's shadow/validation phase explicitly non-patient-facing and additive-only, which is why E2o (cost-of-delay) is held open rather than resolved: the honest residual tension is between 'don't experiment on patients with an unvalidated model' and 'don't withhold a plausibly helpful tool from a high-mortality condition.' Both pull on live alerting and neither is fully retired by the evidence in this packet.

caveats.endorse_only_if: No clinician-facing alert fires before a Northgate-data v2 validation reports pre-registered discrimination and calibration on a held-out local cohort (G1).
A shadow-phase lead-time/leakage audit (G2) demonstrates the model adds warning ahead of, not after, the antibiotic order, and the v2 feature set used at Northgate is documented.
Equity-stratified performance (G3) is reported and acted on, and the model is deployed strictly additively — never as grounds to withhold clinician escalation.
A monitoring dashboard, recalibration cadence, and a pre-defined decommissioning threshold (G4) are live before any pilot alert, with an owner named.

The gating timeline is bounded and the existing clinician-driven sepsis workflow is preserved unchanged throughout, so conditioning the model does not itself become a cause of untreated-sepsis delay (E2).

integrity_verdict: Real challenge: the advocate conceded v1 outright (A1), conceded E1's transferability (A2/B2), and accepted two binding amendments (H2, D1); four BLOCKING objections survived as gated residual risks and lane E's cost-of-delay tension (E2o) was held open, not papered over.
